# Phase 3: PVP crit - Pattern Map

**Mapped:** 2026-07-09
**Files analyzed:** 6 modified (0 new files; `src/module_bindings/` regenerated by tooling)
**Analogs found:** 6 / 6 — every change site has an exact same-repo analog, most shipped by Phase 2 on this very combat path. All line numbers below re-verified against the working tree this session (post-Phase-2 code, commit `aa7116d` era).

> Phase-2 pattern map (`02-PATTERNS.md`) mapped the same combat path; its analogs are refreshed here with current line numbers. This phase is pure composition: re-point `attackPlayer`'s damage input at `resolvePlayerHit`, widen `pvp_hit`, and mirror the `enemy_hit` client rendering idiom onto the PVP event.

## File Classification

| New/Modified File | Role | Data Flow | Closest Analog | Match Quality |
|-------------------|------|-----------|----------------|---------------|
| `spacetimedb/src/index.ts` — `pvp_hit` table +2 columns (542–548) | model (event table) | event-driven / pub-sub | `enemyHit` table (index.ts:569–578) + additive-`.default()` column idiom | exact |
| `spacetimedb/src/index.ts` — `attackPlayer` body rework (1092–1164) | controller (reducer) | request-response | `attackEnemies` (index.ts:1899–1948) + `resolvePlayerHit` (1862–1893) | exact (Phase-2 proved this exact swap on enemies) |
| `src/App.tsx` — subscribe list (122), `pvpHit` onInsert 3-way branch (201–215), `attackPlayer` binding (629–630) | provider (STDB wiring) | event-driven | `enemyHit` useTable + one-subscription comment (227–236, 223–226); `rangedAttack` self-skip (170–175); `sendAttackEnemies` binding (634–635) | exact |
| `src/game/createGame.ts` — `sendAttackPlayer` signature (74), `dealDamage`/`applyRangedProjectileHit`/`applyPvpDamage` (372–440), new `spawnPlayerNumber` handle, `pvpDamageSinceSync` netting (1062) | service (game loop) + component (handle) | request-response + event-driven | `sendAttackEnemies` interface (84–90); `flashRemoteHealth`/`spawnWorldNumber` handle methods (1065–1071); `spawnSelfNumber` netting (1060–1064) | exact (self-rework) |
| `src/game/combat/damageKind.ts` — add `pvpCrit` kind + style | config (style map) | transform | `takenCrit`/`pvp` entries (damageKind.ts:26–27) | exact |
| `src/game/systems/createDamageNumbers.ts` — extend crit `!` label check | component (renderer) | transform | its own `drawNumber` label ternary (createDamageNumbers.ts:53–58) | exact (self-rework, one condition) |

## Pattern Assignments

### `spacetimedb/src/index.ts` — extend `pvp_hit` (model, pub-sub)

**Analog:** `enemyHit` (index.ts:569–578) for the payload shape the client will branch on (`attacker` + `isCrit`); the current `pvpHit` (542–548) is the base being extended.

**Current table** (index.ts:541–548 — keep `target`, `amount`; append two columns):
```typescript
// Broadcasts a PVP hit so the victim's client can float a purple number.
const pvpHit = table(
  { name: 'pvp_hit', public: true, event: true },
  {
    target: t.identity(),
    amount: t.u32(),
  }
);
```

**Target payload analog** — `enemyHit` (index.ts:569–578) carries exactly the two fields being added:
```typescript
const enemyHit = table(
  { name: 'enemy_hit', public: true, event: true },
  {
    attacker: t.identity(),
    positionX: t.f32(),   // pvp_hit needs NO position — identity-anchored (D3-01)
    positionZ: t.f32(),
    amount: t.u32(),
    isCrit: t.bool(),
  }
);
```

**Additive-migrate discipline (D3-01, RESEARCH Pattern 2):** append at END with `.default()` — `attacker: t.identity().default(Identity.zero())`, `isCrit: t.bool().default(false)`. `Identity` is already imported (index.ts:2 `import { ScheduleAt, Identity } from 'spacetimedb';` — verified). Phase-2 learning: `.default()` does NOT make insert optional — the single insert site (index.ts:1117, the only `pvpHit.insert` in the repo) must supply both values in the same edit. If migrate-publish rejects (Open Q1), fallbacks: retry without `.default()`, then empty-table drop+recreate (`enemy_carry` precedent — event tables hold zero rows).

---

### `spacetimedb/src/index.ts` — `attackPlayer` rework (controller, request-response)

**Analog:** `attackEnemies` (index.ts:1899–1948) — the Phase-2-shipped intent reducer this signature mirrors; `resolvePlayerHit` (1862–1893) — the composition to call as-is.

**Signature analog** (attackEnemies args, index.ts:1900–1906) — drop `damage: t.u32()`, keep target, add intent:
```typescript
// attackEnemies (current, shipped Phase 2):
{ centerX: t.f32(), centerZ: t.f32(), radius: t.f32(), isSkill: t.bool(), comboCount: t.u32() }
// attackPlayer becomes (D3-04):
{ targetIdentity: t.identity(), isSkill: t.bool(), comboCount: t.u32() }
```

**Guards stay byte-for-byte** (index.ts:1095–1113): `requirePlayer(ctx)` → target lookup → self-attack → party friendly-fire pass-through → both safe-zone checks → `distanceBetweenPlayers(attacker, target) > MAX_HIT_RANGE` (`MAX_HIT_RANGE = 45`, index.ts:206). Note `attackPlayer` THROWS `SenderError` on guards (unlike `attackEnemies`' silent returns) — keep that; it's pre-existing (RESEARCH Pitfall 7).

**Damage swap** — the ONLY deleted logic is index.ts:1115–1117:
```typescript
// DELETE (client-trusted value + dealt-amount broadcast):
const clampedDamage = Math.min(damage, MAX_HIT_DAMAGE);
const dealt = Math.min(clampedDamage, target.currentHealth);
ctx.db.pvpHit.insert({ target: targetIdentity, amount: dealt });
```
Replace with the exact `attackEnemies` per-hit idiom (index.ts:1917 clamp + 1926–1935 resolve/insert):
```typescript
const combo = Math.min(comboCount, MAX_COMBO_FOR_GEMS);                    // D2-04, index.ts:1917 idiom
const { amount, isCrit } = resolvePlayerHit(ctx, attacker, isSkill, combo, 'melee'); // D3-03: no profile
// D3-02: FULL computed amount, PRE-branch (killing blows float full strength — enemy_hit convention)
ctx.db.pvpHit.insert({ target: targetIdentity, attacker: attacker.identity, amount, isCrit });
const dealt = Math.min(amount, target.currentHealth);
```
`resolvePlayerHit` (index.ts:1862–1893) already contains the whole composition — `CHARACTER_COMBAT` lookup, constellation/transcend, `skillGrantActive` window (D2-03), `computeBaseDamage`, `rollCrit(..., () => ctx.random())`, `resistedDamage` (inert with no profile), and the `MAX_HIT_DAMAGE` clamp (line 1891). Do NOT re-clamp in the reducer.

**Kill path untouched (SC3):** everything from `const remainingHealth = target.currentHealth - dealt;` (1119) through `respawnPlayerAtSpawn` (1158–1162) — including the `[A1]` shard-conservation comment block (1144–1157) — consumes `dealt` exactly as before. Non-fatal hits: `amount === dealt`, so nothing shifts.

---

### `src/App.tsx` — subscription fix + 3-way `pvpHit` branch + binding (provider, event-driven)

**Analog 1 — one-subscription rule:** the `enemyHit` comment block (App.tsx:223–226) documents it in-repo:
```typescript
// enemy_hit is an EVENT table, so it must NOT also appear in the manual
// subscription list above — useTable already opens its own subscription, and a
// second overlapping subscription would deliver each event row twice, firing
// this onInsert twice (two numbers ~0.16u apart). One subscription = one number.
```
`tables.pvpHit` currently violates this: it is in the manual list (App.tsx:122) AND has a `useTable` (App.tsx:201). REQUIRED in this slice: delete line 122's `tables.pvpHit,` entry. (`skillCast`/`rangedAttack`/`healEvent` share the violation — out of scope, flag only.)

**Analog 2 — self-skip idiom:** `rangedAttack` (App.tsx:170–175):
```typescript
useTable(tables.rangedAttack, {
  onInsert: attack => {
    if (attack.attacker.toHexString() === myIdentityRef.current) return;
    gameRef.current?.handleRemoteSkillCast(attack);
  },
});
```

**Analog 3 — the current `pvpHit` callback being reworked** (App.tsx:201–215) — keep both existing behaviors (theft-context mark, health-bar flash) inside the new 3-way branch:
```typescript
useTable(tables.pvpHit, {
  onInsert: row => {
    const targetHex = row.target.toHexString();
    if (targetHex === myIdentityRef.current) {
      lastPvpHitOnMeAtRef.current = performance.now();   // KEEP: shard-theft toast disambiguation (208)
      gameRef.current?.spawnSelfNumber(row.amount, row.isCrit ? 'pvpCrit' : 'pvp');
      return;
    }
    gameRef.current?.flashRemoteHealth(targetHex);        // KEEP: victim bar reveal (212)
    if (row.attacker.toHexString() === myIdentityRef.current) {
      // D3-05: own non-crit already drawn locally in applyPvpDamage — suppress; upgrade crit only.
      if (row.isCrit) gameRef.current?.spawnPlayerNumber(targetHex, row.amount, 'crit');
      return;
    }
    gameRef.current?.spawnPlayerNumber(targetHex, row.amount, row.isCrit ? 'crit' : 'normal'); // spectator (D2-02)
  },
});
```
Suppression lives HERE (App.tsx callback), not `createDamageNumbers` — mirrors the established self-skip idiom; `myIdentityRef` already lives here (RESEARCH discretion answered).

**Analog 4 — reducer binding:** `sendAttackEnemies` binding (App.tsx:634–635) is the shipped intent-binding shape. Current `sendAttackPlayer` (629–630) becomes:
```typescript
// Current:
sendAttackPlayer: (target, damage) =>
  connection.reducers.attackPlayer({ targetIdentity: target.identity, damage }),
// Becomes (mirror of sendAttackEnemies at 634–635):
sendAttackPlayer: (target, isSkill, comboCount) =>
  connection.reducers.attackPlayer({ targetIdentity: target.identity, isSkill, comboCount }),
```
Requires `pnpm run spacetime:generate` after the server signature change (regenerates `src/module_bindings/` — never hand-edit).

---

### `src/game/createGame.ts` — intent send + handle method + netting (service/component)

**Interface analog:** `sendAttackEnemies` (createGame.ts:84–90) is the intent-shaped signature; `sendAttackPlayer` (74) currently `sendAttackPlayer(target: Player, damage: number): void;` → becomes `sendAttackPlayer(target: Player, isSkill: boolean, comboCount: number): void;`. Doc-comment style: copy the `/** … */` block idiom of 79–83.

**Call-site rework** — `applyPvpDamage` (415–440): thread `isSkill` through and swap the send; KEEP the local display number (D3-05):
```typescript
// Current per-hit body (433–437):
remotePlayer.lastPvpHitAt = elapsedSeconds;
hitSomeone = true;
network.sendAttackPlayer(remotePlayer.row, Math.round(damage));            // ← swap to (row, isSkill, combo)
effectSystem.spawnBurst(remotePosition.clone().setY(1.2), 0xff4a4a, 14);   // keep
damageNumbers.spawn(remotePosition.clone().setY(remotePosition.y + 1), damage, kind); // KEEP — D3-05 local display
```
All gates above (421–432: safe zones, party, `PVP_HIT_COOLDOWN_SECONDS` 0.3, `PVP_MAX_HIT_RANGE` 45, `VERTICAL_HIT_GATE`, radius+0.8) stay unchanged. `combo` is in closure scope (registerComboHit, 410–413). The two callers pass `isSkill`: `dealDamage` (384) forwards its own `isSkill` param; `applyRangedProjectileHit` (404) passes `false` (bow = basic). The `damage` param survives as display-only (computed at 501–504 for basic, 572–574 skill multiplier).

**New handle method analog:** `flashRemoteHealth` (1068–1071) for the `remotePlayers` lookup + `spawnWorldNumber` (1065–1067) for the spawn call:
```typescript
// Existing (copy both halves):
spawnWorldNumber(positionX, positionZ, amount, kind) {
  damageNumbers.spawn(new THREE.Vector3(positionX, 1.2, positionZ), amount, kind);
},
flashRemoteHealth(identityHex) {
  const view = remotePlayers.get(identityHex);
  if (view) view.lastPvpHitAt = elapsedSeconds;
},
// New (identity-anchored — D3-01 rationale):
spawnPlayerNumber(identityHex, amount, kind) {
  const view = remotePlayers.get(identityHex);
  if (!view) return;                       // victim despawned — drop silently
  const p = view.model.group.position;
  damageNumbers.spawn(p.clone().setY(p.y + 1), amount, kind);
},
```
Declare it in the game-handle interface next to the existing trio (createGame.ts:126–130: `spawnSelfNumber`/`spawnWorldNumber`/`flashRemoteHealth`).

**Netting guard (RESEARCH Pitfall 2 — load-bearing):** `spawnSelfNumber` (1060–1064) nets PVP damage out of the synced "taken" number:
```typescript
spawnSelfNumber(amount, kind) {
  // Account PvP damage so the synced HP drop isn't re-floated as a "taken" number.
  if (kind === 'pvp') pvpDamageSinceSync += amount;     // ← MUST become: kind === 'pvp' || kind === 'pvpCrit'
  damageNumbers.spawn(playerPosition.clone().setY(playerPosition.y + 1.4), amount, kind);
},
```
Miss this and every PVP crit on you double-floats a phantom red "taken" number via `syncMyServerRow` (1027–1046).

---

### `src/game/combat/damageKind.ts` — `pvpCrit` kind + style (config)

**Analog:** the `takenCrit` precedent — same-color, bigger-scale crit variant of an existing kind (damageKind.ts:25–27):
```typescript
taken: { cssColor: '#ff5c3c', fontScale: 1 },
takenCrit: { cssColor: '#ff5c3c', fontScale: 1.7 },
pvp: { cssColor: '#c07dff', fontScale: 1.15 },
```
Add `'pvpCrit'` to the `DamageKind` union (line 2–10) and `pvpCrit: { cssColor: '#c07dff', fontScale: 1.7 }` to `DAMAGE_KIND_STYLES` — purple stays the "a player hit you" semantic; attacker/spectator crits use the existing magenta `crit` (`#ff2bd6`, fontScale 1.85). The `Record<DamageKind, …>` type makes a forgotten style entry a compile error.

---

### `src/game/systems/createDamageNumbers.ts` — crit `!` label (component, self-rework)

**Analog/target:** its own `drawNumber` label ternary (createDamageNumbers.ts:53–58):
```typescript
const label =
  kind === 'crit' || kind === 'takenCrit'    // ← add: || kind === 'pvpCrit'
    ? `${amount}!`
    : kind === 'heal'
      ? `+${amount}`
      : `${amount}`;
```
One condition added; no other renderer change — `createDamageNumbers` stays a dumb kind-driven renderer.

## Shared Patterns

### Intent-not-value reducer signature
**Source:** `attackEnemies` (index.ts:1899–1907) — shipped Phase 2.
**Apply to:** `attackPlayer` args + `Network.sendAttackPlayer` + App.tsx binding — ONE atomic slice (server signature + regenerated bindings + client callers), per the no-legacy rule; a half-migrated pair is the "no such reducer" failure class.

### Single damage-resolution seam
**Source:** `resolvePlayerHit` (index.ts:1862–1893) — apply order base → × crit → resist → clamp is load-bearing and pure-tested.
**Apply to:** `attackPlayer` calls it with `'melee'` and NO profile (D3-03). Never re-compose damage inline; never re-clamp (clamp is inside, line 1891).

### Full-amount pre-branch event insert (D3-02 / Phase-2 `e368fda` convention)
**Source:** `enemyHit.insert` sites (index.ts:1929–1935, 1955–1961) — insert BEFORE the survivor/fatal branch with the full computed `amount`; HP math uses `Math.min(amount, currentHealth)` separately.
**Apply to:** the `pvpHit.insert` in reworked `attackPlayer`. Phase 2 shipped a bug when the emit sat inside the survivor branch (fatal hits floated nothing) — keep it pre-branch.

### Additive `.default()` column migration
**Source:** the append-at-end + `.default()` rule (02-PATTERNS "Additive .default() column migration"; `player.transcendShards` precedent) — now applied to an event table for the first time (Open Q1: publish is the acceptance test).
**Apply to:** `pvp_hit`'s `attacker`/`isCrit`. `Identity.zero()` for the identity default (import exists, index.ts:2). Update the ONE insert site in the same edit — `.default()` never relaxes the TS insert type.

### One-subscription rule for event tables
**Source:** `enemyHit` comment block (App.tsx:223–226) + `pullResult` slot-dedupe workaround (App.tsx:181–183).
**Apply to:** remove `tables.pvpHit` from the manual list (App.tsx:122) in this slice — otherwise every PVP number (victim purple, attacker crit upgrade, spectator) draws twice.

### Self-skip / own-crit-upgrade `onInsert`
**Source:** `rangedAttack` self-skip (App.tsx:170–175); the attacker-suppression + crit-upgrade shape is the D2-01/D2-02 pattern.
**Apply to:** the reworked `pvpHit` callback (attacker branch: suppress non-crit, upgrade crit at the victim's live position via the new `spawnPlayerNumber`).

### PVP netting kind-set
**Source:** `spawnSelfNumber` netting (createGame.ts:1060–1064) ↔ `syncMyServerRow` taken-number logic (1027–1046).
**Apply to:** any kind that represents "PVP damage shown to the victim" must be in the netting check — `'pvp'` AND the new `'pvpCrit'`.

### Grep-gate + determinism (unchanged constraint)
**Source:** CLAUDE.md rules; `resolvePlayerHit` already routes randomness through `ctx.random`.
**Apply to:** no `Math.random`/`Date.now` in `spacetimedb/src`; this phase adds no new RNG call sites.

## No Analog Found

None — every change site has an exact in-repo analog; Phase 2 shipped this pattern set on the enemy path and this phase transposes it to PVP.

| File | Role | Data Flow | Reason |
|------|------|-----------|--------|
| — | — | — | (all files have same-repo analogs) |

## Notes for the Planner

- **No new files, no new tests expected** (RESEARCH Wave 0: zero gaps). `resolvePlayerHit` inputs are already pure-tested (crit/damage/skillGate suites); `serverSync.test.ts` (INV-5) must stay green with no new mirrored constants.
- **Deploy order is part of the phase:** publish local (additive, NO `--delete-data`, `--module-path spacetimedb`) → `pnpm run spacetime:generate` → `pnpm build` → two-client migrated-DB playtest. The publish itself is the migrate-acceptance test for the event-table `.default()` question.
- **Range envelope:** client `PVP_MAX_HIT_RANGE = 45` (createGame.ts:206, 3D) ⊂ server `MAX_HIT_RANGE = 45` (index.ts:206, 2D XZ) — no conflict; leave both untouched (RESEARCH verdict).
- **Playtest observers:** doubled remote skill/projectile VFX (`skillCast`/`rangedAttack` still double-subscribed) and occasional attacker-only local numbers on server-rejected edge hits are PRE-EXISTING, not Phase-3 regressions.
